skill.name: frontend-design
skill.description: Guides AI assistants to create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with strong visual direction and working implementation details.
skill.summary: Create polished frontend interfaces with bold, context-aware design choices.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: The analyzer reported 16 potential issues, but the referenced SKILL.md lines contain design guidance prose, not executable code or cryptographic operations. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance matches are false positives caused by frontend design terminology. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, network behavior, filesystem access, or command execution was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the cited lines use ordinary frontend design prose. The matches appear to come from words such as name, description, design, distinctive, and implementation guidance. No cryptographic API, hashing function, encryption routine, secret handling, or security-sensitive code appears in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is natural language design guidance, not executable code. I found no evidence of cryptographic algorithms or security-sensitive data processing.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged reconnaissance patterns on lines that discuss interface purpose, typography, and visual details. These lines ask the assistant to understand user needs and choose visual treatments. No commands, host inspection, environment probing, network discovery, or data collection behavior appears in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are descriptive UI design instructions. I found no evidence of system commands, reconnaissance intent, or sensitive host information gathering.

content.value_statement: Generic interface prompts often produce repetitive layouts and weak visual direction. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward polished, context-specific frontend work.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of web components, pages, dashboards, posters, and application interfaces.
Encourages a clear visual direction before implementation begins.
Provides frontend aesthetics guidance for typography, color, motion, composition, and visual details.
Supports HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, and similar frontend implementation contexts.
Helps avoid repetitive AI-generated layouts and common generic design choices.
limitations: It provides design guidance, not a reusable component library or build system.
It does not include assets, templates, scripts, or automated validation tools.
It cannot guarantee accessibility or performance unless the implementing assistant verifies them.
It may need project-specific constraints to match an existing brand or design system.

use_cases.title: Create a memorable landing page
use_cases.description: Turn a product brief into a visually distinct page with clear layout, typography, motion, and implementation direction.

use_cases.title: Improve an internal dashboard
use_cases.description: Guide an assistant to redesign dense operational screens with stronger hierarchy, spacing, and component polish.

use_cases.title: Build polished frontend prototypes
use_cases.description: Generate working UI code that feels intentional, expressive, and matched to the project context.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner page concept
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when starting a simple page from a short idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to create a single responsive page for [product or topic]. Choose a clear visual direction and implement working HTML and CSS.

prompt_templates.title: Component redesign
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when an existing component feels generic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to redesign this [component]. Keep the behavior the same, but improve typography, spacing, color, motion, and visual character.

prompt_templates.title: Application interface build
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when building a richer tool or app screen.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to build a production-quality [app type] interface in [framework]. Include states, navigation, responsive behavior, and a strong aesthetic direction.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced design system pass
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when refining a broader interface language.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to create a cohesive visual system for [project]. Define layout rules, component treatments, motion principles, and implementation details.

output_examples.input: Design a homepage for an experimental music festival.
output_examples.output: A bold editorial page concept with expressive typography, layered visual textures, animated schedule reveals, and responsive section layouts.

output_examples.input: Redesign a finance dashboard that looks too generic.
output_examples.output: A restrained operational interface with improved hierarchy, clearer data groupings, refined color use, and practical interaction states.

output_examples.input: Create a React interface for a collaborative writing tool.
output_examples.output: A polished app shell with document navigation, editor states, collaboration cues, responsive panels, and a distinct visual identity.

best_practices: Provide the audience, product purpose, technical stack, and any brand constraints before asking for design work.
Ask for responsive behavior, interaction states, and accessibility checks when the output must be production-ready.
Request a clear aesthetic direction so the result feels intentional rather than decorative.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a beautiful page without explaining the audience or use case.
Do not use it as a substitute for accessibility testing, usability research, or production QA.
Do not request visual novelty when the product needs strict design-system consistency.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps AI assistants create distinctive frontend pages, components, dashboards, applications, and visual web artifacts.

faq.question: Does it write working frontend code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill instructs the assistant to implement working frontend code when the task requires it.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it include JavaScript packages or scripts?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill contains only SKILL.md guidance and no executable package files.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The static findings were evaluated as false positives in non-executable design prose.

faq.question: When should I avoid this skill?
faq.answer: Avoid it when strict brand compliance matters more than creative visual exploration.
